g.text: NO writes to .db file during txn
NO locks between reader and writer
3 | for f in 0..limit: copy WAL frame to .db
Writer appends frames at end of file
Random DB writes → sequential WAL writes
Commit frame: db_size > 0, marks txn boundary
frame_count | int | total frames in WAL
else → scan WAL backward (frame beyond snapshot)
if empty slot → return -1 (not in WAL)
No locks needed — integer compare only
Recovery rebuilds index by scanning WAL file
Page 0 (byte 0) | DB header (100 bytes) + root page
Page N (byte N*4K) | B-tree pages for user tables
In WAL mode: file is STALE until checkpoint runs
Readers must check WAL before reading this file
WAL | writer appends to .db-wal only (never touches .db mid-txn)
WAL | readers check WAL index first, then .db
WAL | readers and writer run concurrently (no locks)
RJ | writer modifies .db directly (after journaling old pages)
RJ | readers go to .db directly (always current)
RJ | writer holds exclusive lock, blocks all readers
